program_name: High Efficiency Electric Home Rebate Act (HEEHRA)
description: Direct rebate program providing funds for low- and moderate-income households to install efficient electric appliances and related electrical/insulation upgrades.
eligibility_criteria: Income-qualified | Eligible projects: electric clothes dryer, electric stove, heat pump for space heating, heat pump water heater, install new efficient electric appliances, insulation, ventilation, and sealing, upgraded breaker box, upgraded electrical wiring [needs review: expiry unknown]
incentive_amount: $4.5 billion in direct rebates for low- and moderate-income households. Examples: rebate covering full cost of heat pump installation for space heating up to $8,000; up to $1,750 for a heat pump water heater; $840 for an electric stove; $840 for an electric clothes dryer; up to $4,000 for upgraded breaker box; $2,500 for upgraded electrical wiring; $1,600 for insulation, ventilation, and sealing. For moderate-income households same rebates available to cover 50% of costs.

property_type: BATTERY STORAGE TECHNOLOGY (BEGINNING IN 2023, MUST HAVE CAPACITY OF AT LEAST 3 KILOWATT HOURS); FUEL CELLS; GEOTHERMAL HEAT PUMPS; SOLAR ELECTRIC PANELS; SOLAR ROOFING TILES/SOLAR SHINGLES; SOLAR WATER HEATERS; WIND TURBINES
description: A federal tax credit equal to 30% of costs for qualifying residential clean energy property installed between 2022 and December 31, 2025 (subject to specific property limits and qualification rules).
eligibility_criteria: Eligible projects: Existing homes, Main home (primary residence), New homes, Second home (in some cases; must be located in the United States and not rented to others) | You may claim the credit whether you own or rent the home. You cannot claim the credit if you are a landlord or other property owner who does not live in the home. You cannot claim fuel cell property credit for a second home or for a home not located in the United States. Business-use rules: if property is used solely for business you cannot claim the credit; if partly for business, full credit allowed when business use up to 20% and credit based on nonbusiness share when business use is more than 20%. Used (previously owned) clean energy property is not eligible. Labor costs for onsite preparation, assembly, original installation, and necessary piping or wiring may be included. Subsidies, rebates, and other financial incentives may reduce qualified expenses when calculating the credit (public utility subsidies and certain rebates must be subtracted). Interest paid (including loan origination fees) is not included in qualified expenses. | Administered by: Internal Revenue Service (IRS)

property_type: EXISTING HOME; MAIN HOME; PRIMARY RESIDENCE
description: Federal tax credit for qualified energy-efficient improvements to a taxpayer's main home for property placed in service Jan 1, 2023 through Dec 31, 2025. Credit equals 30% of eligible expenses with specified annual and per-item limits; includes qualified energy efficiency improvements, residential energy property, and home energy audits.
eligibility_criteria: Owner-occupied required | Eligible projects: Biomass stoves and boilers, Building envelope components, Central air conditioners, Electric or natural gas heat pump water heaters, Electrical components to support residential energy property (panelboards, sub-panelboards, branch circuits, feeders with 200 amps or more), Exterior doors, Exterior windows and skylights, Heat pumps (electric or natural gas), Home energy audits, Insulation and air sealing materials or systems, Natural gas, propane, or oil furnaces and hot water boilers, Natural gas, propane, or oil water heaters, Qualified energy efficiency improvements, Residential energy property | Applies to your main home located in the United States; must be an existing home you improve or add onto (not a new home). Cannot claim if you are a landlord or if property is used solely for business. If home is used partly for business: business use up to 20% = full credit; business use over 20% = credit based on nonbusiness share. Building envelope components must have expected lifespan of at least 5 years. Insulation and air sealing materials or systems are exempt from qualified manufacturer and PIN requirements. For property placed in service in 2025, no credit unless item was produced by a qualified manufacturer and the taxpayer reports the Qualified Manufacturer Identification Number (QMID) on the return. Subsidies, rebates, or other financial incentives may need to be subtracted from qualified expenses per IRS rules. Credit is nonrefundable and cannot be applied to future tax years. | Administered by: Internal Revenue Service

property_type: BUSINESSES; DWELLINGS; HOMES; INDIVIDUAL RESIDENCES; STRUCTURES; UTILITIES AND INFRASTRUCTURE
description: FEMA’s Hazard Mitigation Grant Program (HMGP) provides funding to state, local, tribal and territorial governments to develop hazard mitigation plans and rebuild in ways that reduce or mitigate future disaster losses in their communities. This funding becomes available following a Presidentially declared disaster.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible projects: Acquiring hazard-prone homes and businesses, Building safe rooms for communities and individual residences, Developing, adopting, evaluating, enhancing and enforcing building codes and standards, Elevating structures above known flood levels, Implementing drainage improvement projects, Installing permanent barriers to prevent floodwater entering homes and businesses (e.g., levees, floodwalls, floodproofing), Planning & Enforcement (creating and adopting hazard mitigation plans), Reconstructing damaged dwellings on elevated foundations, Retrofitting buildings to resist floods, earthquakes, wind, wildfires and other hazards, Stabilizing slopes to prevent structural losses, Upgrading utilities and infrastructure to enhance resistance to natural hazards | Homeowners and businesses are not eligible to apply directly for HMGP grants; local communities may apply for funding on their behalf. To receive HMGP funding, all state, local, tribal and territorial governments must develop and adopt hazard mitigation plans. | Administered by: FEMA [needs review: amount unspecified; expiry unknown]

description: BRIC is a hazard mitigation program that makes federal funds available to states, U.S. territories, federally recognized Tribal Nations, and local governments for hazard mitigation activities, including upgrading and modernizing infrastructure to promote resilience against natural hazards.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible projects: pre-disaster and hazard mitigation projects, relocating critical facilities out of flood areas, school safe rooms, securing pump stations, utility hardening | Eligible applicants and subapplicants: states, U.S. territories, federally recognized Tribal Nations, and local governments. Eligible applicants and subapplicants must apply through the FEMA Grants Outcomes (FEMA GO) portal. The full Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) is published on Grants.gov. Previous subapplications submitted for the initial BRIC funding opportunity published in January 2025 will not be reviewed. | Administered by: Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) [needs review: amount unspecified]
program_links: FEMA Grants Outcomes (FEMA GO) portal; NOFO published on Grants.gov

program_name: Alternative Fuel Vehicle Refueling Property Credit — Individuals (residential)
property_type: BIDIRECTIONAL CHARGING EQUIPMENT; CHARGING STATIONS FOR 2- AND 3-WHEELED ELECTRIC VEHICLES (FOR USE ON PUBLIC ROADS); ELECTRIC VEHICLE CHARGING EQUIPMENT; FUEL DISPENSER; FUEL STORAGE PROPERTY; PROPERTY USED TO STORE OR DISPENSE CLEAN-BURNING FUEL
description: Federal tax credit for qualified alternative fuel vehicle refueling or recharging property installed at a taxpayer's main home. Applies to residential EV charging equipment and other qualified refueling property placed in service Jan 1, 2023–Jun 30, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible projects: installation on property used as a main home, property placed in an eligible census tract, residential EV charger installation placed in service during the tax year | Property must be placed in service during the tax year; original use must begin with the taxpayer; used primarily in the U.S. and U.S. territories; must be in an eligible census tract (low-income community census tract or non-urban census tract) as determined by GEOID lists; credit is subject to recapture if property stops qualifying within 3 full years from placed-in-service date. | Administered by: Internal Revenue Service

program_name: Alternative Fuel Vehicle Refueling Property Credit — Businesses and Organizations (standard)
property_type: BIDIRECTIONAL CHARGING EQUIPMENT; CHARGING STATIONS FOR 2- AND 3-WHEELED ELECTRIC VEHICLES (FOR USE ON PUBLIC ROADS); ELECTRIC VEHICLE CHARGING EQUIPMENT; FUEL DISPENSER; FUEL STORAGE PROPERTY; PROPERTY USED TO STORE OR DISPENSE CLEAN-BURNING FUEL
description: Federal tax credit for qualified alternative fuel vehicle refueling or recharging property placed in service for business or organizational use. Standard rate of 6% with per-item caps applies to property placed in service Jan 1, 2023–Jun 30, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible projects: business or investment use installations (depreciable property), commercial EV charger installation placed in service during the tax year, property placed in an eligible census tract | Property must be placed in service during the tax year; original use must begin with the taxpayer; used primarily in the U.S. and U.S. territories; must be in an eligible census tract (low-income community census tract or non-urban census tract) as determined by GEOID lists; for business or investment use the property must be depreciable; portion of the credit for business or investment use is treated as a general business credit and subject to general business credit rules; basis of property must be reduced by amount of credit; credit is subject to recapture if property stops qualifying within 3 full years from placed-in-service date. | Administered by: Internal Revenue Service

program_name: Alternative Fuel Vehicle Refueling Property Credit — Businesses and Organizations (prevailing wage and apprenticeship enhanced rate)
property_type: BIDIRECTIONAL CHARGING EQUIPMENT; CHARGING STATIONS FOR 2- AND 3-WHEELED ELECTRIC VEHICLES (FOR USE ON PUBLIC ROADS); ELECTRIC VEHICLE CHARGING EQUIPMENT; FUEL DISPENSER; FUEL STORAGE PROPERTY; PROPERTY USED TO STORE OR DISPENSE CLEAN-BURNING FUEL
description: Federal tax credit providing an enhanced 30% credit for qualified refueling or recharging property for businesses and organizations that satisfy prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements, with a $100,000 per-item cap, for property placed in service Jan 1, 2023–Jun 30, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible projects: business or investment use installations (depreciable property), commercial EV charger installation that satisfies prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements, property placed in an eligible census tract | Enhanced 30% rate available only to businesses and organizations that meet prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements; same geographic and placed-in-service requirements apply (eligible census tract, original use with taxpayer, used primarily in U.S. and territories); portion of the credit for business or investment use is treated as a general business credit; basis of property must be reduced by amount of credit; credit is subject to recapture if property stops qualifying within 3 full years from placed-in-service date. Tax-exempt and government entities can claim the credit through elective pay if they notify the seller in writing. | Administered by: Internal Revenue Service

program_name: New Clean Vehicle Credit (Internal Revenue Code Section 30D)
property_type: FUEL CELL ELECTRIC VEHICLE (FCV); NEW PLUG-IN ELECTRIC VEHICLE (EV); OTHER VEHICLES; PICKUP TRUCKS; SPORT UTILITY VEHICLES; VANS
description: Federal tax credit for new qualified plug-in electric and fuel cell vehicles under IRC Section 30D, subject to battery, critical mineral, assembly, MSRP, and income requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Income-qualified | Eligible projects: purchase of a new qualified vehicle (vehicle must be bought new and placed in service) | Must buy for own use (not for resale) and use primarily in the U.S.; modified adjusted gross income (AGI) must not exceed $300,000 (married filing jointly or surviving spouse), $225,000 (head of household), or $150,000 (all other filers) using AGI from the year of delivery or the prior year (whichever is less). Seller must report required information to buyer and to the IRS when buyer takes possession; dealer must provide a time-of-sale report. Vehicle must have battery capacity of at least 7 kWh, GVWR less than 14,000 pounds, be made by a qualified manufacturer, undergo final assembly in North America, and meet MSRP caps ($80,000 for vans/SUVs/pickups; $55,000 for other vehicles). For vehicles placed in service after Sept 30, 2025, the vehicle must have been acquired on or before Sept 30, 2025 (acquisition may be demonstrated by a binding written contract and making a payment on or before Sept 30, 2025). | Administered by: Internal Revenue Service (IRS)
incentive_amount: Credit up to $7,500. For vehicles placed in-service Jan 1–Apr 17, 2023: $2,500 base plus $417 for a vehicle with at least 7 kWh battery capacity and $417 for each kWh beyond 5 kWh (minimum credit stated as $3,751 for 7 kWh). For vehicles placed in-service Apr 18, 2023 and after: credit up to $3,750 if vehicle meets the critical minerals requirement only; up to $3,750 if it meets the battery components requirement only; up to $7,500 if it meets both. A vehicle that doesn't meet either requirement is not eligible.

property_type: CAR; FUEL CELL VEHICLE (FCV); LIGHT TRUCK; PLUG-IN ELECTRIC VEHICLE (EV)
description: Federal tax credit for qualifying previously owned plug-in electric or fuel cell vehicles purchased from a licensed dealer. For vehicles acquired on or before Sept. 30, 2025, the credit is 30% of the sale price up to $4,000. The credit may be transferred to the dealer at time of sale or claimed on the buyer's tax return (file Form 8936). The credit is nonrefundable if not transferred.
eligibility_criteria: Income-qualified | Eligible projects: purchase of a previously owned qualified EV or FCV from a licensed dealer | Must be an individual buyer (not for resale); not the original owner; not claimed as a dependent; not have claimed another used clean vehicle credit in the 3 years before the purchase date. Modified AGI limits: $150,000 for married filing jointly or surviving spouse; $112,500 for head of household; $75,000 for all other filers (use AGI from year of delivery or year before, whichever is less). Vehicle requirements: sale price $25,000 or less (includes dealer fees and certain accessories; excludes taxes and title/registration fees); model year at least 2 years earlier than calendar year of purchase; not previously transferred after Aug. 16, 2022 to a qualified buyer; gross vehicle weight rating under 14,000 pounds; battery capacity at least 7 kWh; for use primarily in the United States. Sale must be from a licensed dealer who provides required time-of-sale information and reports to the IRS; dealers must register online and report the information or the vehicle will not be eligible. Purchases made before 2023 do not qualify. Vehicles must be acquired on or before Sept. 30, 2025 (and placed in service) to be eligible; acquisition can be demonstrated by a binding written contract and payment on or before that date. | Administered by: Internal Revenue Service (IRS)

description: Pinellas County Down Payment Assistance Program provides assistance for down payment and closing costs for eligible homebuyers in unincorporated Pinellas County and eligible service areas, in the form of an interest-free, deferred loan up to $75,000.
eligibility_criteria: Income-qualified | Eligible projects: closing costs, down payment, home purchase | Household must meet income eligibility requirements. All household income of people 18 years and older who will reside in the property must be included. Liquid assets cannot exceed $50,000. Purchase price must be $394,000 or less. Home must be your primary residence. Home must be located in unincorporated Pinellas or a city in an eligible service area. Note: St. Petersburg, Clearwater and Largo have their own down payment assistance programs. Applicants must complete an 8-hour HUD-approved homeownership class and 1-hour one-on-one counseling prior to closing. A HUD Uniform Physical Condition Standard inspection is required. | Administered by: Pinellas County Community Development [needs review: expiry unknown]
